---
title: "eda"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
# =========================================================
# EDA: response distribution
# =========================================================

expr_clin_complete %>%
  count(survived_500d) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = survived_500d, y = n, fill = survived_500d)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of 500-day survival outcome",
    x = "Survived more than 500 days",
    y = "Number of patients"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# EDA: missingness in clinical data
# =========================================================

clinical_missing <- clinical_main %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ mean(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "missing_prop") %>%
  arrange(desc(missing_prop))

clinical_missing %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(variable, missing_prop), y = missing_prop)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Top 20 clinical variables by missingness",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Missing proportion"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# Filter low-count genes
# Keep genes expressed at count >= 10 in at least 20% of samples
# =========================================================

keep_genes <- rowSums(expr_mat_model >= 10) >= ceiling(0.20 * ncol(expr_mat_model))
expr_mat_filt <- expr_mat_model[keep_genes, , drop = FALSE]

dim(expr_mat_model)
dim(expr_mat_filt)
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# Log-transform expression for downstream modelling
# =========================================================

expr_log <- log2(expr_mat_filt + 1)

x_expr_log <- t(expr_log) %>% as.data.frame()

dim(x_expr_log)
summary(as.vector(as.matrix(x_expr_log[, 1:min(20, ncol(x_expr_log))])))
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# Variance filter: keep top 2000 most variable genes
# =========================================================

gene_var <- apply(x_expr_log, 2, var, na.rm = TRUE)

top_n <- min(2000, length(gene_var))
top_genes <- names(sort(gene_var, decreasing = TRUE))[1:top_n]

x_expr_top <- x_expr_log %>%
  select(all_of(top_genes))

dim(x_expr_top)
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# PCA for visual exploration
# =========================================================

pca_expr <- prcomp(x_expr_top, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

pca_df <- as_tibble(pca_expr$x[, 1:2]) %>%
  mutate(
    survived_500d = y_expr
  )

ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, colour = survived_500d)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA of top-variance expression features",
    x = "PC1",
    y = "PC2",
    colour = "500-day survival"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
